zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabell Collett於大約1545年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾費亞黑斯東時，她的父親William Collett29歲，母親Elizabeth26歲。她於1563年8月29日與Leonard Potts在英國越可爾費亞黑斯東結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1575年在她的家鄉享年32歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabell Collett于大约1545年出生在英国英格兰越可爾費亞黑斯東时，她的父亲William Collett29岁，母亲Elizabeth26岁。她于1563年8月29日与Leonard Potts在英国越可爾費亞黑斯東结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1575年在她的家乡享年32岁。

nameMeanings.name: Collett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from a pet form of the personal name Col(l), a pet form of Nicholas . There was also a feminine form, Collette, which may also have given rise to a surname. The name could also mean ‘Coll the younger’.

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: English (southwestern England and south Wales) and Dutch: from the personal name Nicholas (from Latin Nicolaus, from Greek Nikolaos, from nikān ‘to conquer’ + laos ‘people’). Forms with -ch- are due to hypercorrection (compare Anthony ). The name in various vernacular forms was popular among Christians throughout Europe in the Middle Ages, largely as a result of the fame of a 4th-century Lycian bishop, about whom a large number of legends grew up, and who was venerated in the Orthodox Church as well as the Catholic. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Croatian and Serbian Nikolić (see Nikolic ), Greek Papanikolaou ‘(son of) Nicholas the priest’ and Nikolopoulos . Compare Nickolas .

History: The colonial official and revolutionary patriot Robert Carter Nicholas was from a prominent VA family on both sides. His father was a British navy surgeon who emigrated c. 1700 from Lancashire, England, to Williamsburg, VA.

nameMeanings.name: Isabell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabell Collett was born about 1545, in Featherstone, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Collett, was 29 and her mother, Elizabeth, was 26. She married Leonard Potts on 29 August 1563, in Featherstone, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died about 1575, in her hometown, at the age of 32.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabell Collett est née environ 1545, à Featherstone, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Collett, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Leonard Potts le 29 août 1563, à Featherstone, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1575, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 32 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabell Collett um 1545 in Featherstone, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Collett 29 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth 26. Sie hat am 29. August 1563 in Featherstone, Yorkshire, England Leonard Potts geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1575 im Alter von 32 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabell Collett è nata il circa 1545 nel Featherstone, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, William Collett, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Leonard Potts il 29 agosto 1563, nel Featherstone, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1575, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 32 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabell Collettが約1545年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアフエアスエルストーンで生まれた時，父William Collettは29歳，母Elizabethは26歳であった。1563年8月29日にイギリスヨークシュアイアフエアスエルストーンでLeonard Pottsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1575年に故郷で32歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabell Collett(이)가 대략 1545년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 피더스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Collett(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1563년 8월 29일 영국 요르크실르 피더스톤에서 Leonard Potts(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1575년 그녀의 고향에서 32세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabell Collett nasceu aproximadamente 1545, em Featherstone, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William Collett, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Leonard Potts em 29 de agosto de 1563, em Featherstone, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1575, em sua cidade natal, com 32 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabell Collett родилась около 1545 г. в местности фитирстоун, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William Collett было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Leonard Potts 29 августа 1563 г. в местности фитирстоун, еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1575 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 32 лет.
